description: You are a newly-hired compliance analyst. Your first assignment is to prepare a fully auditable Python workspace in /home/user/compliance_project using the standard library module venv. The security team will run an automated script that expects the exact files, paths and data formats described below. Make sure every command you issue in the terminal is also recorded in an on-disk CSV audit trail so that the entire build process can be reconstructed later. 1. WORKING DIRECTORY • Create the base directory /home/user/compliance_project. • All files requested in this task must live inside that directory (or its sub-directories). 2. VIRTUAL ENVIRONMENT • Inside the base directory, create a virtual environment named env with python -m venv. • Do NOT use global interpreters or virtualenv; the directory must be exactly /home/user/compliance_project/env. 3. THIRD-PARTY PACKAGES • After activating the venv, install the **exact** versions below from PyPI: – requests  == 2.31.0 – pandas    == 2.1.4 • No other packages are required, but if the resolver automatically pulls in dependencies that is acceptable. 4. REQUIREMENTS FILE (for reproducibility) • Create /home/user/compliance_project/requirements.txt. • It **must** contain at least the two lines below, one per line, in the exact order shown (other automatically-frozen packages may appear after them): ``` pandas==2.1.4 requests==2.31.0 ``` 5. AUDIT TRAIL • Produce a CSV file called /home/user/compliance_project/audit_trail.csv. • The CSV delimiter is a literal comma (,). • The very first row is the header line shown exactly here (no spaces): timestamp,action,details,status • Each subsequent line represents ONE build step, recorded **immediately after** that step completes. • Minimum required rows after the header (you may add more, but these three must appear in this order): 1. create_venv – log the creation of env. 2. install_packages – log the installation of requests and pandas. 3. freeze_requirements – log the generation of requirements.txt. • The “timestamp” column must be an ISO-8601 date-time string in UTC (e.g. 2024-05-06T14:02:59Z). • The “status” column should be the word success when the step finishes without error; otherwise use failure. • The “details” column should briefly describe what was done (e.g. “env directory created”, “installed pandas==2.1.4, requests==2.31.0”, “requirements.txt written with 2 pinned packages”). 6. VALIDATION SCRIPT (optional but recommended) • You may write a helper script inside the venv that prints “environment OK” if all required files exist and the CSV looks correct. This is for your own assurance; it will not be graded. 7. WHAT THE GRADER WILL CHECK The automated grader will verify all of the following: a. /home/user/compliance_project/env exists and contains a Python interpreter. b. /home/user/compliance_project/requirements.txt exists and contains at least the two exact lines for pandas and requests, in that order. c. /home/user/compliance_project/audit_trail.csv exists and follows the column order and naming described above. d. The CSV has at least the three mandatory action rows in the correct sequence and each has status of success. e. Activating the venv and running python -m pip list shows pandas 2.1.4 and requests 2.31.0. You are free to issue any shell commands necessary, as long as the final on-disk artefacts exactly match what is described.
truth:
<!-- Paths -->
    <baseDir>/home/user/compliance_project</baseDir>
    <venvDir>/home/user/compliance_project/env</venvDir>
    <reqFile>/home/user/compliance_project/requirements.txt</reqFile>
    <auditFile>/home/user/compliance_project/audit_trail.csv</auditFile>

    <!-- requirements.txt: first two lines MUST match exactly -->
    <requirementsExactTop>
pandas==2.1.4
requests==2.31.0
    </requirementsExactTop>

    <!-- audit_trail.csv -->
    <!-- Header line must match exactly -->
    <csvHeader>timestamp,action,details,status</csvHeader>
    <!-- Mandatory action rows and order -->
    <row1action>create_venv</row1action>
    <row2action>install_packages</row2action>
    <row3action>freeze_requirements</row3action>
    <!-- Status for each required row -->
    <status>success</status>

    <!-- Package versions that must appear when pip list is run inside the venv -->
    <packages>
        <package name="pandas" version="2.1.4"/>
        <package name="requests" version="2.31.0"/>
    </packages>
